plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration and meet the single name cap.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to 15% due to its downtrend and to reduce overall exposure.
Reduce SMH to 10% to lower semiconductor downtrend risk.
Increase QQQ to 25% to boost broad index exposure.
Buy ANET at 5% to add a high-quality tech name to the portfolio.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expect reduced upside potential compared to other modes.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration and meet the single name cap.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop and to reduce leverage.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce QQQI to 10% due to its downtrend and to free up capital.
Trim SMH to 8% to lower semiconductor downtrend risk.
Buy ANET at 7% to add a quality tech name.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index, quality tech, and some strong scanner setups. Expect moderate drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration and meet the single name cap.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop; reallocate capital.
Reduce QQQI to 5% due to its downtrend and to free up capital for growth.
Trim SMH to 5% to lower downtrend semiconductor exposure.
Increase CRWD to 18% to lean into an uptrending tech name.
Buy NOW at 10% to add a high-scoring scanner setup.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It uses concentration in high-scoring setups and allows for leveraged ETFs (though SOXL is sold due to its stop). This mode carries significantly higher risk and potential for large drawdowns; daily decay of leveraged ETFs is a factor.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration and meet the single name cap.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 30% to boost income exposure for monthly distributions.
Sell SMH entirely to exit high-beta and downtrending exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 5% to lower high-beta single name exposure.
Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce growth-oriented exposure.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It rotates heavily into income ETFs and broad index, cutting leverage and high-beta names. While aiming for low drawdown, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in selloffs.
